**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Nanjing Boding Information Technology Co., Ltd. presents a composite specimen of extremely exaggerated 'high-capital shellification' and classification disorder. The underlying preset massive Apple Inc. consumer electronics OEM data has been thoroughly cleared by the forensic engine. The official commercial registry system was clearly misled by the 'Technology' in its name and the massive array of hardware vocabulary listed in its license, categorizing it under the heavy-asset 'Computer, Communication and Other Electronic Equipment Manufacturing - Electronic Device Manufacturing.' However, the physical probe directly shattered its false capital moat: this twenty-year-old shell established in 2002 boasts a registered capital of 30 million and a paid-in capital of 5.01 million, but its 2024 insured headcount is surprisingly only '1 person'! Its registered address is 'Room 108-1, 1st Floor, No. 28 Ningshuang Road, Yuhuatai District, Nanjing City,' which is an extremely typical ground-floor small shop or virtual partitioned desk in commercial office buildings around China (Nanjing) Software Valley. In a partitioned single room with a '-1' suffix on the 1st floor, relying on 1 employee to 'manufacture' electronic devices? This completely breaches the lower limits of industrial common sense. Reviewing its 'encyclopedic' business license: from software development and system integration, it goes all the way to cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things, even crossing over into new materials, satellite remote sensing, and metal materials sales. The diagnostic conclusion is extremely clear: this is absolutely not a high-tech manufacturing giant. This is a Micro IT Proxy & Universal Trading Shell cloaked in a 30 million capital facade. The boss (Huang Junyou) utilizes this seemingly powerful old shell to take on Frankenstein projects everywhere around Software Valley, acting as a universal channel for software and hardware procurement, qualification affiliation, and fund-routing invoicing.
